concerns.text: Task T033 proposes implementing a 'variant where ε adapts based on local convergence history'. This directly violates FR-003 which mandates a static sweep of ε ∈ [0.05, 0.50] and contradicts the spec's definition of the experiment. The task introduces a scope-creep feature (adaptive epsilon) not authorized by the spec or plan.

Task T035 ('Rule Space Exploration') requires implementing 'alternative simple update rules (e.g., median-based update instead of mean)'. This violates FR-002 which strictly mandates the implementation of the discrete-time Hegselmann-Krause update rule. The task attempts to validate a philosophical point by altering the core model, which is a constraint violation.

Task T034 introduces a new analysis metric ('degree distribution heterogeneity/variance') not present in FR-006. FR-006 explicitly limits the regression to 'assortativity, average path length' and 'topology type'. Adding degree variance as a primary predictor without spec authorization constitutes a silent narrowing/expansion of the acceptance criteria.

Phase 6 is labeled 'Sensitivity Analysis & Robustness' but includes T033, T034, T035 which are exploratory 'reviewer response' tasks. The spec (FR-008) only authorizes sensitivity analysis on the convergence threshold. The tasks should be explicitly marked as 'Exploratory/Out-of-Scope' or the spec must be amended to include these specific analyses before implementation.
